descriptionHeader: Oceanfront Oasis in the Blue Bay Private Estate

Upstairs, the main bedroom, with its own ensuite bathroom, opens onto a private wooden deck where you can enjoy the serene beauty of the ocean and surrounding landscape. A separate outdoor bedroom also offers an ensuite bathroom and its own balcony, affording picturesque views of the fynbos.

The lower level of the home continues to impress, with two additional ocean-facing bedrooms, each with ensuite bathrooms. One of these bedrooms features a private wooden deck with ground-level ocean views, while the other is a spacious haven that includes space for a lounge setting and pool table, leading out to an undercover patio. The convenience of a large, automated single garage with ample storage space and a laundry area ensures that every need is catered for.

This property also incorporates an array of modern amenities, including a Bosch gas oven and hob, 12 solar panels with battery backup, a solar geyser, an advanced security system, and ambient mood lighting throughout. The main bedroom is equipped with air conditioning and heating, while all other bedrooms have wall-mounted heating panels. With built-in insect screens, roller blinds, and an abundance of built-in cupboards, every detail has been thoughtfully considered.

Located just 4 km from Africa’s southernmost tip and approximately 2 km from the meeting point of the Atlantic and Indian Oceans, this home offers direct beach access. This property in the Blue Bay Private Estate is more than just a home; it is a sanctuary of peace and luxury.

The home is within short walking distance—less than 1 km—from the beach and scenic cliff paths. All the restaurants that Onrus has to offer are mere minutes away, ensuring convenient access to dining and leisure activities.
Located approximately 6 kilometres from Hermanus Central, Onrus has long been a desirable destination for both residential and holiday home buyers. This quaint village is known for its rustic and artistic character, and it continues to attract new residents, including those moving to the Cape from Gauteng, KwaZulu-Natal, the Eastern Cape, Limpopo, and other provinces.
The name "Onrus" translates to "restless," a reference to the constant pounding of the surf along the rocky coastline. Despite the development of security, retirement, and housing complexes in the last decade, Onrus has managed to preserve its old-world charm. The area is still rich in giant Milkwood trees, which offer cooling shade over the historic fishermen’s cottages that have stood for generations.

descriptionHeader: A Masterpiece of Coastal Luxury: Modern Living with Unrivalled Ocean Views in De Kelders

This is more than a home; it is a secure, sustainable, and high-value investment in the Western Cape’s most desirable Whale Coast.

The home’s allure is dramatically enhanced by its potential for growth and investment. Provision has been made and plans were drawn up and approved for the construction of a new second level, which would increase the total under roof area to an impressive 745m² and offer 5 additional en suite bedrooms. The chef’s kitchen is a true statement piece, featuring unique copper-clad cabinetry that combines rustic charm with modern sophistication, equipped with a gas stove and an electric oven. Step onto the undercover verandah to the ultimate outdoor entertainment zone, complete with a built-in braai (BBQ) and a genuine pizza oven, perfect for relaxed al-fresco dining with a panoramic ocean backdrop. Furthermore, for those seeking immediate, hassle-free occupation, the option exists to purchase the home fully inclusive of its bespoke luxury furniture for an additional cost.

Key Features & Investment Value:
•	Bedrooms & Bathrooms: Four spacious bedrooms, each with a luxurious en suite bathroom, ensuring supreme comfort and privacy.
•	Gourmet Kitchen: Unique copper-clad kitchen, gas stove, electric oven, separate scullery, and pantry.

Comprehensive Location Features (De Kelders, Gansbaai): Located in the heart of the Whale Coast, De Kelders offers unparalleled tranquillity and natural adventure. The area is world-renowned for its scenic cliffs and nature reserves, providing exceptional, on-your-doorstep whale-watching opportunities.

descriptionHeader: Hero's Rest (519)
description:
Breath-taking views with endless opportunities, where beauty, history, peace and tranquillity are all moulded together in one exquisite package!

The perfect natural setting, a unique chance to embrace the beauty of nature, while preserving this pristine terrain for generations to come, not to mention the farming section, with two good boreholes, 80ha of flat farming area situated on the foothills of the majestic mountain range, the best of both worlds. The “farming section” is suitable for horses, cattle, game or sheep. Ample water includes the two tested boreholes plus a fresh mountain spring, making self-sufficient farming not only a dream but a reality.

With three legal entry/exit points, off the N2 highway, close to Swellendam town, on the gate-way to the Garden Route. Featuring a unique biodiverse ecosystem, an oasis of fynbos biome, home to many endemic plant species. It encompasses a variety of habitats, including fynbos forest areas, mountain hills, rock formations, pools and the beautiful Hessequas River, which runs through the “Nou-Kloof” on this land, where Piet Lafras Uys, the father of the ever so popular Hero, Dirkie Uys was born and raised.

In the Kloof area, where the Hessequas River flows, you can explore the lush haven on hiking trails over the mountain or through the forest. You will encounter wildlife, such as Klipspringer, Duiker, Vaal Rhebok, Porcupine, Otters and various bird species (e.g. the scarce Secretary Bird!). You can swim or fish in the rock pools. Experience the spectacular starlight nights, which gives you the ultimate sense of calmness and relaxation. Feast your eyes.

The stone house, built on the riverbank has two bedrooms and an open plan living area, with rustic braai facilities. A cosy one-bedroom cob-house is built a bit higher up on the river bank. The views from these houses are just breath-taking! Awaiting a new Owner, with a vision for sustainable lifestyle development such as implementing renewable energy solutions, synergetic farming systems, solar farming, or eco-tourism, due to the excellent location, topography and climate. Balancing conservation and sustainable development ensure a harmonious coexistence with nature.

Tourism is a definite option, offering various opportunities for outdoor enthusiasts. Recreational activities like mountain biking, 4x4 trails, climbing, horse riding, bird watching, hiking, golf, building camp sites, tiny homes, or chalets with hot tubs, for weekend breakaways or holidays will create a popular destination to come.
By conserving and protecting this natural treasure, for whatever purpose, you create a lasting impact and contribute to the global movement of environmental stewardship.

Acquiring as a group or as an individual, is not only an investment, but will contribute to preserving the natural balance for future generations, a legacy you leave behind.

Swellendam is a town located in the Western Cape province of South Africa. It is situated at the foot of the Langeberg Mountains and is approximately 180 km east of Cape Town. Known for its scenic beauty, historic charm, wine, range of restaurants and cafes offering a variety of cuisines, including traditional South African dishes and international flavours. Swellendam and its surroundings have something to offer for every visitor. Easy access to nearby attractions such as the R62 route going inland, De Hoop Nature Reserve, Bontebok National Park, and the Southern tip of Africa, at Agulhas town.

Excluding VAT @ 0%, Sold as a Going Concern.

descriptionHeader: Your newly built Country Lifestyle Estate home
description:
Proudly Presented by Lew Geffen Sotheby's International Realty. 

Your newly built Country Lifestyle Estate home awaits!

Maremmana Estate lies along the banks of the Bot River, approximately 5 km from the village of Bot River. Conveniently located, the estate is only an hour’s drive from Cape Town International Airport and 20 minutes from the bustling seaside town of Hermanus.

The developer, who resides on the estate, has thoughtfully designed this house with the Western Cape weather conditions and seasonal prevailing winds in mind. The plot is in a prime, elevated position, and the single-story house is built to maximize natural light and stunning panoramic views.

The bedroom wing, covering 82.5m², features a main bedroom with an en-suite bathroom and two additional bedrooms that share a bathroom. All rooms open onto a generous stoep that runs along the south side of the house, overlooking a beautiful dam.

The northern wing, covering 111.5m², features an open-plan living room, dining room, and kitchen area, complete with a scullery and laundry, as well as a double garage. The wings are connected via a 16.5m² enclosed stoep area, which is flooded with natural light, and a large central outdoor stoep that is sheltered from the prevailing southeast wind in summer.

Each 400m² plot in the estate is surrounded by an additional 600m² exclusive-use area, offering a sense of space and farm-style living.

The house boasts premium finishes, including:
Laminated wood flooring in bedrooms 2 and 3, as well as the lounge. Carpet in the main bedroom. 
Quartz carpet flooring in the bathrooms.
LED lighting throughout the house.
A solar system with a 7kW inverter and 5kW batteries.
Double-glazed aluminum doors and windows.
A 12kW enclosed wood-burning fireplace.
Melawood kitchen cupboards and built in cupboards in the bedrooms with medium density fiberboard doors. 
A more detailed list of finishes, along with approved plans, is available on request.

This is a rare opportunity to secure a newly built home in a secure estate that offers the charm of remote farm-style living while being conveniently close to major towns.

descriptionHeader: Luxurious 6-Bedroom Home with Captivating Finishes & Sea Views – Franskraal
description:
Step into a world of timeless elegance and superior craftsmanship in this architecturally designed, double-storey 6-bedroom, 4.5-bathroom, 6-garage residence, ideally located in the serene heart of Franskraal. Created with the discerning buyer in mind, this exquisite home offers a seamless fusion of sophistication, expansive living spaces, and unparalleled functionality — all just a leisurely stroll from the area’s pristine beaches.

From the moment you step inside, you’re greeted by a home that celebrates the art of fine living. A standout feature of this luxurious property is the private library downstairs, offering a tranquil retreat for reading, reflection, or quiet moments of escape. High-end finishes and beautifully curated interiors are accentuated by bespoke touches such as handcrafted sleeper wood doors, door frames, and balustrade handrails — all meticulously crafted from rare African sleeper wood. This remarkable residence provides a luxurious coastal lifestyle for those who appreciate architecture, thoughtful design, and effortless elegance.

The interior exudes a sense of spaciousness and warmth, offering six generously sized bedrooms and 4.5 bathrooms. Three of the bedrooms enjoy the luxury of stylish en-suite bathrooms, while a fourth full bathroom is conveniently shared by three additional bedrooms. A guest toilet ensures ease and comfort for visitors. With a combination of sleek floor tiles and warm PVC flooring, the home is both practical and refined.

At the heart of the home lies a modern kitchen that is as functional as it is stylish, with stunning granite countertops, ample cabinetry, and a dedicated pantry for additional storage. Just off the dining area, you’ll discover a spectacular enclosed entertainment space, perfect for relaxed socializing. With folding stacker doors that seamlessly open to a beautiful garden complete with fruit-bearing grapevines, this space is designed for effortless indoor-outdoor living. It’s complemented by a built-in braai, prep bowl, and basin, making it an entertainer’s dream.

A second, expansive entertainment room is designed for both comfort and style, featuring a built-in braai, a convenient kitchenette, and stacker doors that open to a balcony offering distant sea views. Several other balconies, including four leading off the bedrooms, allow you to fully embrace the peaceful surroundings and enjoy the calming sea-facing vistas. There is also a large hobby room, complete with its own private balcony upstairs, perfect for creative projects or relaxation.

Wine connoisseurs will appreciate the custom wine storage racks nestled beneath the staircase, adding both charm and functionality. Energy-efficient solutions, along with backup power (detailed below), and garaging for six vehicles — all on remote — make this home not only luxurious but also practical and self-sufficient.

Whether you’re seeking a luxurious permanent residence, a stylish holiday retreat, or a property that combines space, character, and elegance, this one-of-a-kind home fulfils every desire.

descriptionHeader: New build of an exquisite north-facing home in Berghof Estate with panoramic views.
